title: senecio integrifolius subsp. aucheri
intro: Senecio integrifolius subsp. aucheri, also known as Aucher's Jerusalem sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and the Middle East. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aucher's Jerusalem sage include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
